Global (All).title: Russian hackers suspected of breaching US federal agency
Global (All).summary: Russian hackers are suspected of breaching a US federal agency's network using a sophisticated phishing campaign, compromising sensitive personal data.

Global (All).title: EU considers measures to reduce reliance on US dollar in Russia-Ukraine conflict
Global (All).summary: The EU is considering measures to reduce its reliance on the US dollar in response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine, which could have far-reaching consequences for global trade and finance.

Global (All).title: Protest erupts in China over COVID-19 lockdowns, restrictions
Global (All).summary: Protests have erupted in several Chinese cities over the government's strict COVID-19 lockdowns and restrictions, which have sparked widespread discontent and calls for democratic reforms.

United States.summary: US Federal Investigation Launched into Twitter Data Breach
US Racial Tensions Rise Amid Protests
US Supreme Court to Rule on Climate Change Case

South Korea.summary: South Koreans stage protest against the proposed military intelligence bill, which critics say infringes on privacy rights and the freedom of the press.
North and South Korea exchange gunfire along the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ), with both sides reporting damage to each other's positions.
South Korea announces a plan to strengthen its military presence in the South China Sea to counter China's increasing activities in the region.

Japan.title: Tokyo Reports 1,000+ New COVID-19 Cases Amid Rise in Omicron Subvariants
Japan.summary: A rise in Omicron subvariants is causing a surge in COVID-19 cases in Tokyo, with over 1,000 new cases reported.

Japan.title: Japan to Introduce 3-Year-Old Children to English Lessons in Public Schools
Japan.summary: The Japanese government has announced plans to introduce English lessons in public schools starting from 2027, which will benefit over 1 million children.

Japan.title: Typhoon Survivors in Japan Face Widespread Power Outages, Flood Damage
Japan.summary: Residents of northeastern Japan are facing power outages and flood damage in the aftermath of Typhoon Noru.
